name: Crane Bag
description: The Crane Bag (Irish: Cran Bagán) is a legendary bag in Irish mythology, traditionally made from the skin of a crane. It is associated with Manannán mac Lir, the sea god, and often depicted as holding a collection of powerful and magical objects. These objects vary depending on the story, but commonly include items like the alphabet of Ogma (Ogham writing), various treasures, or even entire armies. The bag itself is a symbol of knowledge, wisdom, and power contained and controlled. It represents the potential for both great good and great destruction, depending on who wields its contents. Its origins are shrouded in mystery, but it is often linked to the Otherworld and the Tuatha Dé Danann, the mythical race of gods and goddesses. The Crane Bag is more than just a container; it's a repository of cultural heritage and a source of immense magical ability, a testament to the power of knowledge and the responsibility that comes with it. Stories involving the Crane Bag emphasize the importance of understanding and respecting the forces it contains. The contents of the bag are sometimes said to be infinite, representing the limitless possibilities of the Otherworld and the potential for magic to shape reality. Possession of the Crane Bag is often a sign of great destiny or a test of character, as only those who are worthy can properly wield its power.
subtitle: A bag of holding, knowledge, and power
origin: Gift from Manannán mac Lir or crafted by him.
currentLocation: Unknown, lost to time or hidden in the Otherworld.

keyMyths.title: The Alphabet in the Crane Bag
keyMyths.description: The Ogham alphabet was said to be stored within the Crane Bag, highlighting its connection to knowledge and writing. This emphasizes the bag's role as a vessel for cultural and intellectual treasures.

keyMyths.title: Manannán's Gift
keyMyths.description: Manannán mac Lir is often credited with either creating or gifting the Crane Bag to a worthy individual. This highlights Manannán's role as a provider of magical artifacts and knowledge.

sources.author: Peter Berresford Ellis
sources.description: Comprehensive overview of Celtic mythological figures and objects.
sources.text: Comprehensive overview of Celtic mythological figures and objects.

sources.author: Lady Gregory
